body_txt: Sometimes, when a combat scene becomes a bit too vocal and noisy... it stops sounding like fighting. The grunts and yelps and groans start to sound less like hostility and more like rough sex. Related to Cat Fight (and the possible cause of the related Fanservice ). Of course, if you have a character that's Too Kinky to Torture , the moans may be genuine pleasure. While in some cases this may be deliberate, interviews with actual voice actresses and vocal directors indicate that many seemingly clear cases of this are actually a case of Pareidolia , as the voice actress was nether directed to give such an impression nor actually trying to do so. Manipulative Editing can also be used to achieve this as well, by presenting the same sounds outside of their original context. Humans naturally make various grunts or noises reflexively when exerting effort, and, uh, there are various forms of exertion . Similarly, humans also make various noises when experiencing pain. Screaming like a character has been set on fire or a Screaming Warrior can be presented as screaming of a different kind . Video game dialogue is perhaps particularly prone to being repurposed for this, since in many games the Player Character has combat dialogue, which typically includes various sounds when taking action such as hitting a monster, or being hit, or making an effort when exploring the world, such as climbing a wall. The audio files are almost always stored individually and are mixed in the game engine, compare that to say a movie where all the dialogue and sound effects are usually going to be blended together. For those occasions when the characters actually are getting turned on, see Sex Is Violence , Combat Sadomasochist , and Interplay of Sex and Violence .
